title: UroMOCA and ColoMOCA implantation protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"76fa9","text":"This protocol describes the implantation of our bladder (UroMOCA) and bowel device (ColoMOCA) in pigs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Anesthetize/prep the animal for surgery. Aseptic procedures by trained personnel are utilized during the surgery. All tools are sterilized prior to insertion. Drapes will be used and personnel performing surgery will wear clean surgical scrubs, sterile gowns/gloves and masks.
Make a midline suprapubic incision ~9 cm long to expose the bladder, peritoneal cavity and viscera.
Make a single, small incision into the colon.
Insert device through the small incision and secure in place with sterile non-absorbable sutures.
Wake the coloMOCA with radio if not already awake. Confirm data transmission.

Close the colon with an absorbable suture in an interrupted pattern Close the peritoneum and fascia with absorbable sutures using a continuous suture pattern. Close the subcutaneous tissue and skin with non-absorbable monofilament suture with a cutting needle, using an interrupted suture pattern.Skin staples may also be used.
Confirm data transmission from both the UroMOCA and ColoMOCA, and then put devices to sleep
Image to verify placement of the sensors.
Place and zip the jacket on the pig while still anesthetized.
Stay with the animal until fully awake. Return to pig room.
Insert single lumen 8 Fr catheter, and drain bladder. Save some urine for urinalysis.
Isolate bladder and incise the bladder dome.
Close bladder incision and evaluate integrity.
Place sterile drape on abdomen for UroMOCA transmission coil.
Verify function of UroMOCA in vivo.
Isolate the region of the colon where the implant will go.
creator: Brett Hanzlicek
